summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Aurea Controle Financeiro app. All 13 backend pytest cases pass after fixing a MongoDB ObjectId serialization bug in POST /api/installments/purchases. Frontend login (demo-wendy), dashboard (Saldo €1.470,00 + all cards + evolution + category chart), Despesas Compartilhadas (Mercado €222, Marilia/Nathalia €74 each) and Acertos (Te deve €74,00 each) all render correctly.

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to ObjectId in insert_many-mutated docs being serialized. Fixed by popping _id from inst_docs after insert_many.

issues: Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warnings on initial mount - ResponsiveContainer parent likely has no explicit height before first paint. Cosmetic only, charts render correctly.

action_items: (Optional) Set minHeight on ResponsiveContainer parent divs to silence Recharts warnings.
Apply the same _id-after-insert_many pattern review to any other future bulk inserts.
critical_code_review_comments: compute_splits uses round() per participant - rounding diff applied only to last entry on equal split (handled correctly: 222/3=74.0 exact).
Receivables toggle endpoint uses 'received' status while ReceivableIn defaults pending - consistent.
JWT secret is loaded from env (no hardcoded fallback) - good.
No password/min-length validation on register or change-password - may want to enforce min length 6+.
No rate limiting / brute force lockout on /api/auth/login (playbook mentions 5-fail lockout) - not implemented.
test_credentials: wendy@demo.com / demo123, marilia@demo.com / demo123, nathalia@demo.com / demo123
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed (Mercado €222 shared, Wendy personal txs: salary 2500, rent 850, transport 120, cinema 60 = balance 1470).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py covers auth, transactions privacy, installments, receivables, groups, shared-expenses, settlements, dashboard, change-password. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py -v. Installments 500 bug was fixed in server.py by removing _id from inst_docs after insert_many.
rca_of_the_issue: POST /api/installments/purchases returned 500. RCA: motor's insert_many mutates input documents to inject ObjectId _id; FastAPI then failed to serialize ObjectId in the response. Fix: pop('_id', None) from each doc in inst_docs after insert_many before returning.
